questions.text: A graphic image with a resolution of \( 800 \times 600 \) pixels and 24-bit color depth will result in what file size in KB?
questions.explanation: Calculation: \( \frac{800 \times 600 \times 24}{8} = 1,440,000 \) bytes. To convert to KB: \( \frac{1,440,000}{1,024} \approx 1,406.25 \text{ KB} \).

questions.text: What is the file size (in MB) of a 30-second audio clip recorded at 48 kHz, 16-bit resolution, in stereo?
questions.explanation:
File size = \( \frac{\text{Sample Rate} \times \text{Bit Depth} \times \text{Channels} \times \text{Time}}{8} \). 
\( \text{Size} = \frac{48,000 \times 16 \times 2 \times 30}{8} = 5,760,000 \text{ bytes} \). 
Convert to MB: \( \frac{5,760,000}{1,024 \times 1,024} \approx 5.49 \text{ MB} \).

questions.text: Calculate the file size (in GB) of 2 minutes of uncompressed HD video (1280 x 720 resolution, 24-bit color, 30 fps).
questions.explanation:
1. Frame size: \( 1,280 \times 720 \times 3 \text{ bytes} = 2,764,800 \text{ bytes/frame} \). 
2. Total frames: \( 2 \times 60 \times 30 = 3,600 \text{ frames} \). 
3. Total size: \( 2,764,800 \times 3,600 = 9,953,280,000 \text{ bytes} \). 
4. Convert to GB: \( \frac{9,953,280,000}{1,024^3} \approx 9.27 \text{ GB} \).

questions.text: What is the file size (in KB) of a 15-second sound recording with a sample rate of 11.025 kHz, 8-bit depth, and monophonic sound?
questions.explanation:
Calculation: \( \frac{11,025 \times 8 \times 1 \times 15}{8} = 165,375 \text{ bytes} \). 
In KB (binary): \( \frac{165,375}{1,024} \approx 161.5 \text{ KB} \).

questions.text: Calculate the file size in KB for an icon with dimensions \( 32 \times 32 \) and a color depth of 32 bits.
questions.explanation:
Total bits: \( 32 \times 32 \times 32 = 32,768 \text{ bits} \). 
Bytes: \( 32,768 / 8 = 4,096 \text{ bytes} \). 
KB: \( 4,096 / 1,024 = 4 \text{ KB} \).

questions.text: What is the size of a bitmap image in KB if the resolution is \( 100 \times 50 \) and the color depth is 48 bits?
questions.explanation:
1. Total bits: \( 100 \times 50 \times 48 = 240,000 \text{ bits} \). 
2. Total bytes: \( 240,000 / 8 = 30,000 \text{ bytes} \). 
3. KB (binary): \( 30,000 / 1,024 \approx 29.30 \text{ KB} \).

questions.text: A 12-second audio recording is sampled at 22.05 kHz with 16-bit resolution in stereo. The file size is approximately:
questions.explanation:
\( \text{Size} = 22,050 \times 2 \text{ bytes} \times 2 \text{ channels} \times 12 \text{ seconds} = 1,058,400 \text{ bytes} \). 
Convert to MB: \( \frac{1,058,400}{1,024 \times 1,024} \approx 1.01 \text{ MB} \).

questions.text: If a 5-minute recording is made with a sample rate of 44.1 kHz, 8-bit depth, and mono type, what is the file size in MB?
questions.explanation:
Calculation: \( 44,100 \times 1 \text{ byte} \times 1 \text{ channel} \times 300 \text{ seconds} = 13,230,000 \text{ bytes} \). 
In MB: \( \frac{13,230,000}{1,024 \times 1,024} \approx 12.62 \text{ MB} \).

questions.text: How many distinct colors can a 4-bit image display?
questions.explanation: Color depth is calculated as \( 2^n \) where n is the number of bits. \( 2^4 = 16 \).

questions.text: A graphic with resolution \( 1024 \times 768 \) at an 8-bit color depth will yield a file size of:
What is the approximate size of a recording with these parameters in KB? Duration 8s, Sample rate 8 kHz, Bit rate 8, Type Mono.
questions.explanation:
\( 8,000 \times 1 \text{ byte} \times 1 \times 8 \text{ seconds} = 64,000 \text{ bytes} \). 
In decimal KB: \( 64,000 / 1,000 = 64 \text{ KB} \)  ; Binary KB: \(62.5\).

questions.text: What is the binary representation of the web color <#0, #F, #0>?
questions.explanation: #0F0 is shorthand for #00FF00. In 24-bit binary, Red (00) is 00000000, Green (FF) is 11111111, and Blue (00) is 00000000.

questions.text: What is the hexadecimal representation of the 24-bit color binary: 11111111 11111111 11111111?
questions.explanation: Binary 11111111 is FF in hexadecimal. Therefore, the sequence represents White: <#FF, #FF, #FF>.

questions.text: Determine the size of a bitmap image in Bytes given a resolution of \( 40 \times 30 \) pixels and a color depth of 24 bits.
questions.explanation: \( 40 \times 30 \times (24 / 8) = 1,200 \text{ pixels} \times 3 \text{ bytes/pixel} = 3,600 \text{ bytes} \).

questions.text: True or False: In a standard 24-bit RGB model, the intensity of each color component (Red, Green, Blue) is defined by an integer ranging from 0 to 255.
questions.explanation: In an 8-bit per channel system, there are 256 possible values, which are indexed from 0 up to 255.

questions.text: Calculate the file size in KB for a bitmap image with a resolution of \( 20 \times 10 \) and a color depth of 32 bits.
questions.explanation:
\( 20 \times 10 \times 4 \text{ bytes (32 bits)} = 800 \text{ bytes} \). 
KB (binary): \( 800 / 1,024 \approx 0.78 \text{ KB} \).

questions.text: What is the size (in GB) of 1 minute of uncompressed video with a resolution of 1920 x 1080, 24-bit color, at 60 fps?
questions.explanation:
1. Frame size: \( 1920 \times 1080 \times 3 = 6,220,800 \text{ bytes} \). 
2. Total frames: \( 60 \text{ seconds} \times 60 \text{ fps} = 3,600 \text{ frames} \). 
3. Total size: \( 6,220,800 \times 3,600 = 22,394,880,000 \text{ bytes} \). 
4. Convert to GB: \( \frac{22,394,880,000}{1,024^3} \approx 20.86 \text{ GB} \).

questions.text: The Red Book standard for CD audio specifies a sampling rate of 44.1 kHz and 16-bit stereo. What is the approximate file size of 1 minute of this audio?
questions.explanation:
\( 44,100 \times 2 \text{ bytes} \times 2 \text{ channels} \times 60 \text{ seconds} = 10,584,000 \text{ bytes} \). 
In MB: \( \frac{10,584,000}{1,024 \times 1,024} \approx 10.09 \text{ MB} \).

questions.text: How many colors are possible in a High Color (16-bit) image?
questions.explanation: Color depth is calculated as \( 2^{16} = 65,536 \).

questions.text: What is the file size in KB of an image with resolution \( 320 \times 240 \) and 16-bit color depth?
questions.explanation:
Size = \( 320 \times 240 \times 2 \text{ bytes} = 153,600 \text{ bytes} \). 
KB: \( 153,600 / 1,024 = 150 \text{ KB} \).

questions.text: Calculate the file size in KB for 10 seconds of speech audio sampled at 8 kHz, 8-bit, mono.
questions.explanation:
\( 8,000 \times 1 \text{ byte} \times 1 \times 10 = 80,000 \text{ bytes} \). 
Decimal KB: \( 80,000 / 1,000 = 80 \text{ KB} \). Binary KB: \( \approx 78.1 \text{ KB} \). 80 KB is the standard decimal representation.

questions.text: Convert the following binary color string to hexadecimal: 11000011 01010101 00001111.
If you double the bit depth of an audio recording from 8-bit to 16-bit while keeping other parameters the same, the file size will:
questions.explanation: File size is directly proportional to bit depth. Doubling the bits per sample doubles the total data.

questions.text: Find the file size (in MB) of a 10-second video clip with resolution 640 x 480, 24-bit color depth, and a frame rate of 30 fps.
questions.explanation:
1. Frame size: \( 640 \times 480 \times 3 = 921,600 \text{ bytes} \). 
2. Total frames: \( 10 \times 30 = 300 \text{ frames} \). 
3. Total bytes: \( 921,600 \times 300 = 276,480,000 \text{ bytes} \). 
4. MB: \( \frac{276,480,000}{1,024 \times 1,024} \approx 263.67 \text{ MB} \).

questions.text: What is the file size in KB of a black and white (1-bit) image with a resolution of \( 500 \times 500 \) pixels?
questions.explanation:
Total bits: \( 500 \times 500 \times 1 = 250,000 \text{ bits} \). 
Bytes: \( 250,000 / 8 = 31,250 \text{ bytes} \). 
KB (binary): \( 31,250 / 1,024 \approx 30.52 \text{ KB} \).
